seo.title: Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents in Flowise Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix sensitive data leakage prevention in ai agents in Flowise agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In Flowise, sensitive data leakage in AI agents commonly occurs when chatflows process user inputs containing PII through unvalidated LLM nodes or custom tools that echo back raw data without sanitization. Drag-and-drop RAG pipelines often retrieve and expose sensitive documents from vector stores without access controls, while multi-agent handoffs lose sanitization context between nodes. This creates silent leakage paths that are difficult to trace in the low-code environment.

howItManifests.scenario: Chatflow chains producing unvalidated intermediate outputs
howItManifests.symptom: LLM Response Parser node outputs raw user PII in debug logs
howItManifests.impact: Customer emails and phone numbers exposed in Flowise logs and chat history

howItManifests.scenario: Vector store retrieval returning low-relevance documents
howItManifests.symptom: Document Retriever node returns internal employee records for customer queries
howItManifests.impact: Proprietary data mixed into customer responses without filtering

howItManifests.scenario: Custom tool nodes failing silently without error propagation
howItManifests.symptom: Database query tool returns unredacted user records without validation
howItManifests.impact: Direct PII exposure through tool responses passed to final LLM

howItManifests.scenario: Multi-agent sequential flow losing context between agent handoffs
howItManifests.symptom: Agent 1 extracts PII → Agent 2 receives raw data without sanitization flags
howItManifests.impact: Cascading leakage across agent boundaries in sequential flows

detection.name: PII Regex Scanner for Chatflow Outputs
detection.description: Scan all node outputs for common PII patterns using regex across chatflow execution

detection.name: Vector Store Document Leak Detector
detection.description: Monitor Document Retriever nodes for sensitive document retrieval using metadata tags

detection.name: Agent Handoff Context Leak Monitor
detection.description: Track PII propagation between sequential agent nodes in multi-agent flows

fixes.name: PII Redaction Middleware for All Nodes
fixes.description: Universal redaction filter that wraps all chatflow node outputs before downstream processing

fixes.name: Vector Store Access Control Filter
fixes.description: Filter retrieved documents by sensitivity metadata before LLM processing

fixes.name: Agent Context Sanitizer for Handoffs
fixes.description: Clean PII from agent outputs before passing to next agent in sequence

vexAutomation.heading: Automating Sensitive Data Leakage Prevention in AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly as a callback handler in Flowise chatflow configuration, automatically scanning every node execution for PII patterns, vector store sensitivity violations, and agent handoff leaks. It provides real-time blocking of leaky flows and generates remediation reports with exact node locations and leakage paths. The platform learns from your specific data patterns to reduce false positives over time while maintaining zero-trust validation of all AI agent outputs.

With a single npm install and callback registration, Vex monitors production chatflows without code changes. It captures full execution traces including intermediate node outputs that Flowise logs miss, providing complete visibility into leakage vectors. Automated remediation includes dynamic flow rewrites that inject sanitization nodes exactly where needed.

cta.heading: Detect and Fix This Automatically with Vex
cta.description: Add one callback handler to protect all your Flowise agents from sensitive data leakage forever.
